comment.body:
Has anyone here built time-series models? Been looking into overfitting and how to prevent it, and Dr. Earnest Chan did about a 45min lecture about it, and said to instead test a trading strategy on a time series model instead of backtesting and permutations etc. the premise being modeling time-series data  based on market data will not result in overfitting whereas just modeling on past market data and its permutations does create overfitting. 

Struggling to find how to model time-series data though. Wondering if anyone here has done it or knows how to. Thank you!

comment.body:
You might be better off just building a reinforcement learning model vs time series based linear regression model. The market is stochastic and dynamic by nature. If you just train on past market data you will overfit your model. There is a paper that was published last year that compared various ML models for predicting stock indices. Drawback of timeseries is less effective in volatile conditions, does not take into account sequential relationship of data etc. This year thanks to orange man we got massive volatility and 100 point candles every 10mins in April, that will throw off your Time series model. Vibe coding a trading bot is all the rage now and it is much easier compared to years past. Lots of github repos also available with code. There are time series indicators available on Tradingview along with linear regression ones including the linear regression channel that Trading view has part of its standard indicators. Not sure how much additional juice you would get out of custom building one. 

https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0169207023000729


Good luck if you do end up building one and hope you unlock some additional edge.

comment.body:
Thank for this! I’ll be reading this today. From what I understood from him, modeling a trading system on past data DOES cause overfitting and risk (and manipulation of said data). However, his point was that building a time-series model based on past data then testing your trading system on the time series model itself won’t overfit. I’ll post the video here and you can see what I’m saying. 

You can in theory create an infinite number of different paths in said model to test the trading strategy on while the model still preserves things like vol clustering, diffusions, etc. maybe I’m misunderstanding what he was saying (I’ll watch it again haha) but that’s what I got. 

I just finished the basic math models for my trading model, next phase is actually implementing those ML algos and methods into it haha so perfect timing

comment.body: I would agree with the take that if you build a TS model on past data and then test your system or make the TS model an input of your system and have a feedback loop that corrects any mismatches you will have a better trading model.
